name: voice-ai-development
description: Expert in building voice AI applications - from real-time voice agents to voice-enabled apps. Covers OpenAI Realtime API, Vapi for voice agents, Deepgram for transcription, ElevenLabs for synthesis, LiveKit for real-time infrastructure, and WebRTC fundamentals. Knows how to build low-latency, production-ready voice experiences. Use when: voice ai, voice agent, speech to text, text to speech, realtime voice.
capabilities: Design and implement real-time voice AI applications using OpenAI Realtime API
Build voice agents with Vapi platform for phone and web deployments
Implement speech-to-text with Deepgram's streaming transcription
Create natural voice output with ElevenLabs synthesis
Optimize voice pipelines for low latency and seamless user experience
Handle interruptions and implement barge-in detection for natural conversations
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Security analysis confirms this is a legitimate voice AI development skill. Static findings for external_commands, env_access, and weak_crypto are all false positives - the scanner misidentified markdown code fences as shell commands and placeholder API key patterns as actual secrets. No malicious code detected.
valueStatement: Voice AI applications require careful orchestration of speech-to-text, language models, and text-to-speech. This skill provides expert guidance on building responsive voice agents using OpenAI Realtime API, Vapi, Deepgram, and ElevenLabs.
limitations: Latency depends on network conditions and provider response times
Voice quality varies based on audio input quality and provider selection
Cost accumulates per minute of voice processing
Complex multi-provider setups require careful error handling

useCases.targetUser: Support teams and product managers building automated customer service
useCases.title: Customer Support Voice Agent
useCases.description: Build an interactive voice agent that handles customer inquiries, routes calls, and provides 24/7 support using Vapi and Deepgram.

useCases.targetUser: Developers building interactive voice interfaces for applications
useCases.title: Real-Time Voice Assistant
useCases.description: Create a low-latency voice assistant using OpenAI Realtime API for natural conversational experiences in web or mobile apps.

useCases.title: Accessibility Voice Interface
useCases.description: Add voice input and output to existing applications to improve accessibility for users who prefer voice interaction.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Voice Agent Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me set up a basic voice AI agent using Vapi. I want to create a simple agent that can answer questions about my product using the OpenAI model and ElevenLabs voice.
promptTemplates.scenario: Getting started with voice AI development

promptTemplates.title: OpenAI Realtime API Integration
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to implement the OpenAI Realtime API for a voice application. I need to handle audio input from a microphone, send it to GPT-4o, and stream the audio response back.

promptTemplates.title: Latency Optimization
promptTemplates.prompt: My voice agent feels slow. The user speaks, then there's a long pause before the response. How can I optimize my pipeline to reduce perceived latency? I'm using Deepgram for STT and ElevenLabs for TTS.

promptTemplates.title: Interruption Handling
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I implement barge-in detection so users can interrupt the voice agent while it is speaking? I want the agent to stop immediately when the user starts talking.

bestPractices: Always stream audio at every stage of the pipeline - STT interim results, LLM token streaming, and TTS chunk output
Start TTS generation before the LLM finishes responding to minimize perceived latency
Implement proper interruption handling with VAD to detect when users want to speak
antiPatterns: Waiting for complete STT transcription before sending to LLM - this adds seconds of latency
Ignoring user interruptions - failing to stop TTS when user starts speaking creates frustrating conversations
Using a single provider for everything - mixing specialized providers (Deepgram for STT, ElevenLabs for TTS) yields better results

faq.question: What is the difference between OpenAI Realtime API and Vapi?
faq.answer: OpenAI Realtime API provides native voice-to-voice capabilities integrated with GPT-4o. Vapi is a platform that simplifies voice agent deployment with pre-built infrastructure, phone support, and easier configuration. OpenAI gives more control; Vapi provides faster setup.

faq.question: How do I reduce latency in my voice application?
faq.answer: Key strategies: stream all components (STT, LLM, TTS), start TTS before LLM completes, use fast models (nova-2 for Deepgram, turbo for ElevenLabs), deploy near users, and implement proper audio chunking.

faq.question: Can I use my own voice with ElevenLabs?
faq.answer: Yes, ElevenLabs allows you to upload voice samples to create a custom voice clone. You need clear audio samples of the target voice. This requires consent from the voice owner.

faq.question: How do I handle multiple users simultaneously?
faq.answer: Each user session requires a separate WebRTC connection. Track session state per user, manage concurrent API connections, and implement proper scaling infrastructure like LiveKit for handling multiple voice streams.

faq.question: What is barge-in and why does it matter?
faq.answer: Barge-in allows users to interrupt the voice agent while it is speaking. It matters because waiting for the agent to finish is frustrating. Implement using Voice Activity Detection (VAD) to detect user speech and immediately stop TTS output.

faq.question: Which providers work best together?
faq.answer: A common high-quality combination: Deepgram for STT (fast, accurate), ElevenLabs for TTS (natural voices), and OpenAI or Anthropic for LLM. Each provider excels at its specific task.
